---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)

```

```{css}
  body {
    font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
    margin: 20px;
    color: #333333;
  }

  p {
    font-size: 16px;
    line-height: 1.6;
  }
```

## This dynamic reports tracks the average number of reps over all workout as well as the average number of reps recorded for each muscle group.

The data is being recorded through Google Forms and each entry in the data records the workout split day, training phase (cut, bulk, or maintain),
muscle group targeted, and total reps completed.


```{r}
library("tidyverse")

#Reading the csv data
logged_data <- read.csv('https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vTY4_5kwhVp13jYYKbmGPyy4wVaSVkgJ3Bx8jE5gjCBHx3DVrLGhzff1cSKfpCon2oi2u3LR3ROLfZb/pub?gid=1388521915&single=true&output=csv')

#Renaming the columns of logged_data into shorter form
latest_data <- rename(logged_data,
                      "split_day" = 2,
                      "training_phase" = 3,
                      "muscle_group" = 4,
                      "rep_count" = 5)

```


```{r}
#Summary Stats Chunk

#Average number of reps recorded through all workouts
mean_rep <- round(mean(latest_data$rep_count),2)
#Average rep recorded per muscle group
avg_rep_per_muscle <- latest_data |>
  group_by(muscle_group) |>
  summarise(
    mean_reps = round(mean(rep_count, na.rm = TRUE),2),
    count     = n()
  )
```

## Summary
`r paste0("The average reps being recorded per workout is ", mean_rep, " reps")`

`r paste0(avg_rep_per_muscle$muscle_group, ": ", round(avg_rep_per_muscle$mean_reps,1), " reps", collapse = ", ")`

## Bar Charts

```{r}
#Chart of total count of muscle group targeted for each specific workout day 
ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = split_day, fill = muscle_group)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "Muscle Groups Trained Against Workout Day",
    subtitle = "Total count of every mucle group targeted for each workout day over the whole split",
    x     = "Workout Day",
    y     = "Count",
    fill  = "Muscle Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#Chart of average rep logged per muscle group
avg_rep_per_muscle |>
  ggplot(aes(x = muscle_group, y = mean_reps)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "seagreen") +
  labs(
    title = "Average Reps per Muscle Group",
    subtitle = "The average reps recorded for each muscle group over the split",
    x     = "Muscle Group",
    y     = "Average Reps"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


![](https://c.tenor.com/-qBsG1HwR4oAAAAC/tenor.gif)
